nameMeanings.name: Pipard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Lucien, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Michel, Normand, Adrien, Antoine, Benoit, Elzear, Fernand.

French, Walloon, Catalan, and German; Breton (also Le Picard): from Old French and Catalan picard ‘Picard’, a habitational name for someone from Picardy in northern France. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium. Compare Pecore .
French: in some cases, probably also from pic ‘pick, pike’ + the pejorative suffix -ard, applied as a nickname for e.g. a user of a pointed tool.

variants.name: Picart
meanings: French; Breton (rarely Le Picart): variant of Picard , especially in Brittany (Finistère).

variants.name: Pecore
meanings: Altered form of French Picard , reflecting the characteristic Canadian and American French rounding of the -ard ending.

variants.name: Pickard
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire) and German: habitational name for someone from Picardy in northern France (see   Picard   1).

English: in some cases, possibly also from the Old French personal name Picard, probably from an ancient Germanic name, composed of the elements bic ‘sharp point, pointed weapon’ + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’.

nameMeanings.name: RICHARD
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

en.lifeSummary: RICHARD Pipard was born in Kingham, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom as the son of WILLIAM Pipard. He had at least 1 daughter. He died after 1424, in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: RICHARD Pipard est né à Kingham, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de WILLIAM Pipard. Il a eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé après 1424, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: RICHARD Pipard wurde in Kingham, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von WILLIAM Pipard geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb nach 1424 in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: RICHARD Pipard è nato il nel Kingham, Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di WILLIAM Pipard. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia. Morì dopo il 1424, nel sua cittadina.

ko.lifeSummary: RICHARD Pipard(은)는 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 킨그함에서 WILLIAM Pipard의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1424년 이후 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: RICHARD Pipard nasceu em Kingham, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filho de WILLIAM Pipard. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu após 1424, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: RICHARD Pipard родился в местности кингхам, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. Его отца звали WILLIAM Pipard. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер после 1424 г. в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: RICHARD Pipard nació en Kingham, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hijo de WILLIAM Pipard. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija. Murió después de 1424, en su pueblo.
